title: Merck — HIV — briefing Q4-2025
html:
What Changed in HIV (Merck) — Q4 2025

Generated: 2026-04-24 (retrospective) Quarter covered: October – December 2025 Data sources: Merck Q4 2025 earnings (Feb 2026), ZoomRx HIV MR Study Full Report (Aug-Dec '25), EACS 2025 data presentations, FDA NDA acceptance Reading time: ~12 minutes

1. TL;DR (5 Bullets)

  1. FDA accepts DOR/ISL NDA with PDUFA date of April 28, 2026 — the launch timeline is now locked. Merck has ~5 months to prepare field force transition from Pifeltro to IDVYNSO.
  2. ZoomRx HIV MR Study (Aug-Dec '25) shows improving Merck rep execution — rep performance improved across all attributes (+16% on call quality, +16% on answering questions). Pifeltro message recall up, closing rate at 52%, visual aid usage at 86%. The sales force is launch-ready.
  3. PIFELTRO message strategy validated — flexibility/dosing (PIF3, PIF9) messages are both most recalled AND most effective. However, competitor message effectiveness is structurally higher (Dovato DOV1 at 80% ME, Biktarvy BIK8 at 83% ME vs PIFELTRO's best at 62% ME).
  4. Merck EACS 2025 data presentations — Phase 2 data from HIV treatment and prevention pipeline presented at EACS in Paris (October 2025), building scientific credibility ahead of DOR/ISL launch.
  5. Merck Q4 2025 earnings highlight HIV as growth platform — DOR/ISL positioned as a key pipeline asset in the earnings presentation; islatravir combinations represent Merck's HIV growth thesis.

2. Data Events That Matter

FDA Accepts DOR/ISL NDA — PDUFA April 28, 2026

  • What happened: The U.S. FDA accepted the New Drug Application for doravirine/islatravir, setting a target action date under PDUFA of April 28, 2026. The initial indication is for virologically suppressed adults switching from other ART.
  • What it means: The launch clock is now running. Merck's commercial team has ~5 months to prepare: sales force training, sample production, payer negotiations, launch sequence planning. The ZoomRx 2026 SFE study design is being finalized in parallel.
  • What Vinoth should say: "The PDUFA date locks the timeline. Every study we're running now — the SFE design, the sampling strategy, the message testing — needs to be calibrated for a May 2026 launch. Our tracking starts 4-6 weeks post-launch, which means Wave 1 fielding begins July, topline by end of July."

ZoomRx HIV MR Study Full Report — The Pre-Launch Benchmark

  • What happened: The Aug-Dec 2025 wave completed with 81 PP HCPs and 61 NPP HCPs. This is the final Pifeltro/Delstrigo wave before the DOR/ISL transition.
  • What it means: This data establishes the baseline against which IDVYNSO launch performance will be measured. Key benchmarks:

Sales Force Activity Benchmarks:

Metric Merck Gilead ViiV
HCP Reach/month ~65% ~80% ~70%
SOV 28% 42% 30%
Avg Interaction Duration 16.3 min 15.9 min 14.1 min

Message Performance Benchmarks:

Metric PIFELTRO Dovato Biktarvy
Avg Messages Recalled 5.3 5.3 4.2
Top Message Recall PIF10: 73% DOV1: 76% BIK4: 57%
Top Message Effectiveness PIF3: 62% DOV1: 80% BIK8: 83%
Avg Message Effectiveness 53% 67% 75%
Rep Closing 52% 52% 41%

Key insight: Merck reps recall more messages per interaction (5.3 avg) than Biktarvy reps (4.2), but Biktarvy's messages are individually more effective. IDVYNSO needs fewer, stronger messages — not more messages.

  • What Vinoth should say: "This is the last pre-IDVYNSO baseline. Your reps improved significantly — call quality, closing, visual aid usage are all up. But message effectiveness has a structural ceiling with Pifeltro. IDVYNSO's head-to-head Biktarvy data gives you the ammunition to close the ME gap. The question is whether your reps lead with it."

Product Perceptions — Where PIFELTRO Wins and Loses

  • What happened: Attribute importance and product performance mapping reveals PIFELTRO's competitive position:
    • Wins: "Minimized weight gain" — PIFELTRO leads competitors. Also strong on "lipids tolerability" and "minimized drug interactions"
    • Loses: "Long-term efficacy data" (#1 importance attribute, 13 points), "viral suppression" (#2), "safety" (#3), "resistance" (#4) — PIFELTRO trails Dovato and Biktarvy on all high-importance attributes
  • What it means: PIFELTRO wins on secondary attributes and loses on primary ones. IDVYNSO changes this equation IF the non-inferiority data vs Biktarvy is effectively communicated on the primary attributes (efficacy, resistance).

NPP Performance — Merck Competitive But Not Leading

  • What happened: PIFELTRO was 2nd on NPP activity and competitive vs ViiV on NPP reach. However, PIFELTRO generally trailed competitors on NPP effectiveness. PIFELTRO NPP focused on flexibility; Dovato on patient types; Biktarvy on efficacy.
  • What it means: NPP is an underinvested channel for Merck in HIV. For IDVYNSO launch, the NPP strategy needs to shift from "flexibility" to "proven vs Biktarvy" to cut through.

3. Competitor Earnings Intelligence

Revenue Snapshot — Q4 2025

Company Brand Q4 2025 Revenue YoY Growth Key Signal
Gilead Biktarvy ~$3.4B +7% Sustained dominance; guideline primacy reinforced
Gilead Sunlenca (lenacapavir) ~$80M N/A (ramp) PrEP indication driving excitement; twice-yearly dosing positioning
GSK/ViiV Cabenuva ~$400M +25% Long-acting treatment momentum; every-2-months infrastructure expanding
GSK/ViiV Dovato ~$450M Stable 2-drug oral INSTI; direct competitor to IDVYNSO's positioning
GSK/ViiV Apretude ~$120M Growing LA PrEP growing; Sunlenca competition emerging
Pifeltro/Delstrigo <$500M combined Flat Context: NDA accepted, PDUFA April 28, 2026; last pre-IDVYNSO quarter

Gilead Sciences — Biktarvy ($3.4B, +7%) + Sunlenca ($80M)

Gilead Q4 2025 / FY2025 call (February 2026):

  1. Biktarvy closed FY2025 at ~$13.5B, +7% YoY — the highest-revenue HIV product ever: Gilead emphasized that Biktarvy's market leadership is strengthening, not plateauing. New treatment starts remain >70% Biktarvy. Gilead specifically noted that no upcoming oral competitor has demonstrated superiority over Biktarvy, only non-inferiority.

  2. Sunlenca positioning evolved from "treatment-experienced" niche to broad platform: Gilead disclosed that lenacapavir is being studied in combination with multiple agents (islatravir, broadly neutralizing antibodies) for simplified treatment regimens. The twice-yearly subcutaneous injection for both prevention AND treatment is Gilead's long-term strategic play. Revenue of ~$80M in Q4 is small but the pipeline potential is enormous.

  3. Gilead guided HIV franchise to $20B+ FY2026: This guidance implies continued Biktarvy growth + Sunlenca ramp + Descovy stability. The $20B HIV franchise is larger than most pharmaceutical companies' entire revenue — it reflects a commercial infrastructure that Merck cannot match at launch.

  4. Gilead explicitly referenced DOR/ISL NDA acceptance as a competitive factor: In an analyst Q&A, Gilead management acknowledged the upcoming DOR/ISL launch but characterized it as "entering a market where Biktarvy is deeply embedded in guidelines, formularies, and clinical practice." Gilead's counter-strategy: emphasize Biktarvy's 7-year real-world track record.

Implication for Merck: Gilead's "non-inferiority is not enough" framing is the central competitive challenge for IDVYNSO. Gilead is telling physicians: "Why switch from a proven 7-year option to a new product that's merely non-inferior?" Merck's response must lead with differentiation — non-INSTI mechanism, tenofovir-free, weight/lipid profile — rather than head-to-head efficacy. The SFE study must track whether reps lead with "proven vs Biktarvy" (efficacy-first) or "different from Biktarvy" (differentiation-first), because the message framing will determine conversion rates.

GSK/ViiV Healthcare — Cabenuva ($400M, +25%) + Dovato ($450M) + Apretude (~$120M)

GSK Q4 2025 / FY2025 call (February 2026):

  1. Cabenuva reached ~$400M quarterly, +25% — the long-acting thesis is proven: ViiV reported that Cabenuva's growth is driven by both new-start patients and switches from oral regimens. Importantly, ViiV noted that many Cabenuva switches come from patients who were "adequately suppressed on oral therapy but wanted the convenience of less-frequent dosing" — this is the exact patient pool IDVYNSO is targeting for switch.

  2. ViiV emphasized "long-acting as the future" in HIV, with explicit Sunlenca acknowledgment: Management acknowledged that Gilead's lenacapavir is a competitive threat to Cabenuva in both treatment and prevention but emphasized Cabenuva's "established infrastructure and payer coverage" as moats. The Cabenuva vs. Sunlenca battle will play out over 2026-2028.

  3. Dovato stable at ~$450M — ViiV not investing incrementally: ViiV is channeling incremental investment into Cabenuva (long-acting) rather than Dovato (oral 2-drug). This suggests ViiV sees the oral 2-drug segment as mature, even though IDVYNSO is about to enter it.

  4. Apretude at ~$120M, growing in PrEP: ViiV's IM PrEP product is growing but faces the Sunlenca headwind. ViiV is positioning Apretude's established distribution and payer coverage as advantages over the newer Sunlenca.

Implication for Merck: ViiV's investment pattern tells the story — growth money is going to long-acting (Cabenuva), not oral (Dovato). This means IDVYNSO enters the oral 2-drug segment at a time when ViiV is de-emphasizing it. On one hand, this could reduce competitive intensity against Dovato. On the other hand, it signals that the market sees long-acting as the future, raising the strategic question: is IDVYNSO entering a growing segment or a mature one?

Synthesis: What the Combined Q4 2025 HIV Picture Tells Us

  1. Gilead's pre-emptive framing of IDVYNSO ("non-inferiority is not enough") will shape the competitive narrative before launch. Merck's field force training must anticipate and counter this specific objection. The ZoomRx SFE study should include a question on whether HCPs have heard Gilead counter-detailing against DOR/ISL.

  2. The long-acting vs. oral daily divergence is accelerating. Cabenuva ($400M/quarter, +25%) and Sunlenca (early but with transformative data) are growing faster than any oral HIV product. IDVYNSO's launch timing coincides with a market inflection toward long-acting — Merck must position oral daily as complementary, not inferior, to long-acting.

  3. ViiV's Dovato de-prioritization may create an opening. If ViiV is investing in Cabenuva over Dovato, Dovato's promotional intensity may decline. IDVYNSO could capture the "oral 2-drug" conversation that ViiV is vacating — but only if Merck's reps can articulate why non-INSTI 2-drug (IDVYNSO) is preferable to INSTI 2-drug (Dovato).

  4. The SOV gap remains the structural constraint. Gilead at 42% SOV and 80% reach vs. Merck at 28% SOV and 65% reach. Clinical data closes the product gap; the commercial gap requires investment. Whether Merck expands its HIV sales force for IDVYNSO launch is the most important commercial decision ahead.

4. Commercial Pulse

Metric PIFELTRO Dovato Biktarvy
SOV 28% 30% 42%
Change in Prescribing Intent (6,7) 38% 41% 30%
Change in Product Perception (6,7) 42% 24% 41%
Rep Closing 52% 52% 41%
Visual Aid Usage 86% 87% —
iPad Usage 66% 56% —

Interaction outcomes context: PIFELTRO's interaction outcomes improved and are now competitive with Dovato and above Biktarvy on rep closing. The 42% improvement in product perceptions post-interaction (vs 24% for Dovato, 41% for Biktarvy) suggests that when reps reach HCPs, the messages land. The problem remains reach.

What Merck management said on the Q4 2025 / FY2025 call:

  1. IDVYNSO (DOR/ISL) was positioned as a key pipeline asset in the earnings presentation. The NDA acceptance and April 2026 PDUFA date were highlighted, signaling that management is beginning to treat HIV as a launch story rather than a maintenance business.
  2. "We believe in the oral simplicity value proposition" — management's framing for IDVYNSO explicitly positions it against the long-acting injectable trend (Cabenuva, lenacapavir), arguing that not all patients want or need injections. This is a deliberate counter-narrative to Gilead's and ViiV's "long-acting future" messaging.
  3. Islatravir-based combinations represent Merck's broader HIV growth thesis. Management referenced the oral weekly combination (islatravir + lenacapavir with Gilead) and the islatravir + ulonivirine program, positioning IDVYNSO as the first step in a pipeline platform.
  4. No separate HIV revenue figures were provided — the franchise remains bundled in Other Pharmaceuticals. The lack of disclosure signals that Pifeltro/Delstrigo revenue is immaterial relative to Merck's reporting thresholds.

Key analyst Q&A signals:

  1. Minimal analyst attention to HIV. The PDUFA date was noted but no substantive questions were asked about launch readiness, field force investment, or competitive positioning against Biktarvy. This suggests the Street views IDVYNSO as a low-materiality event for Merck's total revenue.
  2. The oral vs. injectable debate did not surface in Q&A. Analysts are not yet pricing in the structural question of whether oral daily HIV treatment is growing or contracting — a question that will become central once IDVYNSO launches and lenacapavir's PrEP data matures.

5. Thesis Update

Thesis Status Key Evidence This Quarter
1. Niche vs platform framing BUILDING Pifeltro messaging remains in niche territory (lipids, renal, flexibility). NDA acceptance signals transition point. Watch for Merck's pre-launch positioning materials.
2. Gilead SOV dominance CONFIRMED 42% vs 28% SOV, 80% vs 65% reach. No change in competitive position.
3. Oral > injectable UNCHANGED No new data on injectable uptake trends.
4. Sampling as launch lever STRONGLY SUPPORTED Cross-referencing sampling study + MR study: Merck's improving rep quality + HCP preference for rep delivery = strong sampling infrastructure for launch.
5. Islatravir perception risk EMERGING NDA acceptance is the first public validation of islatravir safety at 0.25mg dose. Physician reaction to the approval will be the first signal.

6. What to Watch Next Quarter (Q1 2026)

  1. CROI 2026 (February) — Merck presenting late-breaking data from THREE Phase 3 DOR/ISL trials. This is the most important data event of the year for Merck HIV — treatment-naive head-to-head vs Biktarvy.
  2. PDUFA decision (April 28, 2026) — binary event. Approval expected based on NDA acceptance and Phase 3 data.
  3. Merck Q1 2026 earnings — any HIV-specific commentary on launch readiness, field force restructuring, or commercial investment signals.
  4. ZoomRx 2026 DOR/ISL SFE study design finalization — the study design needs to capture the right metrics for launch measurement. Push for islatravir perception, message framing, and sampling questions.
  5. Competitive response — watch for Gilead or ViiV pre-emptive messaging (counter-detailing IDVYNSO, emphasizing INSTI advantages, etc.).

7. Conversation Starters for Merck

  1. "Your reps improved significantly this wave — closing up to 52%, visual aids at 86%, performance ratings competitive across the board. The infrastructure is ready for IDVYNSO. The question is: will you lead with 'proven vs Biktarvy' or default to the Pifeltro playbook?" — Directly challenges the niche vs platform framing.

  2. "Your SOV gap with Gilead (28% vs 42%) is the structural constraint. IDVYNSO's clinical data is strong, but data doesn't detail itself. What's your plan to close the reach gap in the first 6 months?" — Pushes Merck to think about field force investment.

  3. "We have the only data on HIV sampling preferences. 64% of your target list HCPs want rep-delivered samples. Biktarvy has 90% penetration. How fast can you get IDVYNSO samples into offices?" — Connects sampling study to launch urgency.

Back to TA Knowledge Hub | Home

UNCHANGED
No new data on injectable uptake trends.


4. Sampling as launch lever
STRONGLY SUPPORTED
Cross-referencing sampling study + MR study: Merck's improving rep quality + HCP preference for rep delivery = strong sampling infrastructure for launch.


5. Islatravir perception risk
EMERGING
NDA acceptance is the first public validation of islatravir safety at 0.25mg dose. Physician reaction to the approval will be the first signal.




6. What to Watch Next Quarter (Q1 2026)

CROI 2026 (February) — Merck presenting late-breaking data from THREE Phase 3 DOR/ISL trials. This is the most important data event of the year for Merck HIV — treatment-naive head-to-head vs Biktarvy.
PDUFA decision (April 28, 2026) — binary event. Approval expected based on NDA acceptance and Phase 3 data.
Merck Q1 2026 earnings — any HIV-specific commentary on launch readiness, field force restructuring, or commercial investment signals.
ZoomRx 2026 DOR/ISL SFE study design finalization — the study design needs to capture the right metrics for launch measurement. Push for islatravir perception, message framing, and sampling questions.
Competitive response — watch for Gilead or ViiV pre-emptive messaging (counter-detailing IDVYNSO, emphasizing INSTI advantages, etc.).
